channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: JI Chief Calls on Govt to Reduce Petroleum Levies Amid Rising Prices
item.description: On Tuesday, Jamaat-i-Islami Emir Hafiz Naeemur Rehman strongly criticised the federal government over the steep rise in petroleum prices, which he said is putting a heavy burden on ordinary citizens. ...

item.title: Rahul Gandhi Criticizes PM Modi's Foreign Policy, Accuses Him of Being 'Compromised'
item.description: In a recent statement, Rahul Gandhi, the leader of opposition in the Lok Sabha, called Prime Minister Narendra Modi's approach to India's foreign policy a 'personal' policy and dubbed it a 'universal ...

item.title: Dobson urges Stormers to improve decision-making ahead of Edinburgh clash
item.description: Stormers coach John Dobson has called for a reduction in errors and better decision-making as his team prepares to face an improved Edinburgh squad in Cape Town. Despite a hard-fought 29-21 victory ov...

item.title: Niger Tornadoes boost confidence ahead of Abia Warriors game after win over Rivers United
item.description: Niger Tornadoes dey enter their next Nigeria Premier Football League game against Abia Warriors with serious confidence after dem beat Rivers United 1–0 for Minna. The Ikon Allah Boys manage collect t...

item.title: Congress expresses concern over Pakistan's possible role as mediator in US-Iran conflict
item.description: The Congress party in India has expressed serious concern over reports suggesting that Pakistan may be acting as an intermediary in communications between the United States, Israel and Iran. According...

item.title: Kenya Sends First Shipment to China Benefiting from Zero-Tariff Policy
item.description: On Monday, Kenya officially flagged off its first consignment of exports to China under the newly implemented zero-tariff arrangement. The shipment, which includes fresh avocados, avocado oil, hides a...

item.title: Cake Picnic arrives in Melbourne with 1,600 cakes shared for fun, not competition
item.description: Australia’s first Cake Picnic, part of the Melbourne Food and Wine Festival, attracted a crowd of passionate bakers and cake lovers. Held at Kings Domain, the event featured 1,600 cakes, with particip...

item.title: Nigerian graduate in the US dies after police chase crash in Tennessee
item.description: A 23-year-old Nigerian woman, Oluwalayomi ‘Layo’ Fadero, sadly lost her life in Nashville, Tennessee, after a fleeing driver crashed into her car. The suspect, 52-year-old Ray Eugene Padgett, was said...

item.title: Marcos Declares National Energy Emergency Amid Global Oil Disruptions
item.description: President Ferdinand R. Marcos Jr. has declared a state of national energy emergency in the Philippines following global oil supply disruptions. In response to this crisis, he signed Executive Order No...

item.title: UK to cap veterinary prescription fees and introduce pricing reforms
item.description: In response to rising veterinary costs, the UK's Competition and Markets Authority (CMA) has announced that prescription fees from vets will be capped at £21, with a £12.50 fee for additional medicati...

item.title: Kajido County Focuses on Drug Abuse, Teen Pregnancies, and School Dropouts
item.description: Kajido County is facing a tough time, manze. There's a worrying rise in drug abuse and teen pregnancies within schools, and it's leading to a lot of students dropping out. The county government is now...

item.title: Motsoaledi Prepared to Set Up Tribunal for Gauteng Hospital Deaths
item.description: Health Minister Aaron Motsoaledi has stated that he is ready to establish a tribunal should any parties dispute the findings of the health ombudsman’s investigation into two deaths in Gauteng. The dea...

item.title: Land Ownership and South Africa's Struggle for Proper Settlement
item.description: South Africa is grappling with recurring natural disasters, particularly floods, worsened by climate change. Over the years, provinces like KwaZulu-Natal, the Eastern Cape, Mpumalanga, and Limpopo hav...

item.title: 60-Year-Old Woman Delivers Healthy Baby After Challenging Pregnancy
item.description: A 60-year-old woman in Hanoi, Vietnam, has given birth to a healthy baby after a risky pregnancy, which was complicated by health issues like hypertension, diabetes, and a history of gynecological sur...

item.title: Australian Defence Force ‘Resistance to Interrogation’ training under scrutiny for abuse claims
item.description: Investigations have revealed that Australian Defence Force (ADF) personnel were subjected to severe abuse during Resistance to Interrogation (RTI) and Conduct After Capture (CAC) training exercises. T...

item.title: Zanzibar and the U.S. Discuss Strengthening Ties in Key Sectors
item.description: On March 23, 2026, Zanzibar's President, Dr Hussein Ali Mwinyi, met with U.S. Acting Ambassador Andrew Lentz to discuss enhancing bilateral cooperation in health, climate change, and trade. The two le...

item.title: India to Hold All-Party Meeting Over Growing West Asia Crisis
item.description: The central government has called an urgent all-party meeting to discuss the escalating tensions in West Asia. This development comes in light of rising concerns about an LPG crisis and unstable marke...

item.title: Kim Jong Un confirms North Korea's nuclear stance, warns of severe consequences for South Korea
item.description: In a stern policy speech, North Korea's leader Kim Jong Un reiterated that his country will never abandon its nuclear weapons program, claiming it is now an irreversible step. Kim further declared tha...

item.title: Philippine Sports Hall of Fame welcomes its newest inductees
item.description: The Philippine Sports Hall of Fame is set to honor a new batch of outstanding athletes who have made significant contributions to Philippine sports. This initiative continues to celebrate the legacy o...

item.title: Survey reveals public concern over UK's war preparedness and calls for increased national resilience
item.description: A recent poll has highlighted growing concern among the UK public about the possibility of war within the next five years, with 40% of respondents believing a conflict could occur. More than two-third...
